channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: CubeSandbox: Tencent Cloud Open-Sources an Ultra-Fast Secure Sandbox for AI Agents
item.description: Sandboxing Untrusted Code: Meet CubeSandbox As AI agents become capable of writing, compiling, and running code dynamically, a major security issue has surfaced: how to run this code safely. If a...

item.title: From Optimization to Protection: Adding a Security and Governance Agent to Your Snowflake Multi-Agent Team (Part 3)
item.description: From Optimization to Protection: Adding a Security and Governance Agent to Your Snowflake Multi-Agent Team (Part 3) In Part 1, we built an Admin Agent for usage and cost visibility. In Part 2, we...

item.title: Why I Love the Word "Pivot"
item.description: One of my favorite words in the startup and product-building world is pivot. For a long time, I thought a failed project meant wasted time. Today, I see it differently. Every project I worked...

item.title: Building Picturesque AI: one studio, 50+ models, and the plumbing nobody wants to maintain
item.description: One creative studio for images, video, music, audio, editing, upscaling, and motion control. 50+ models, one credit balance. This is mostly about how we built it and what went wrong along the way....

item.title: How to Create a Skill in Claude Code
item.description: This is a cross-post — the original (and any updates) live at broke2builtai.com. The first time I watched Claude Code reach for a skill I hadn't told it to use — read a folder, run the script...

item.title: Our Journey to GSSoC 2026: Omnikon's Repository Has Been Selected! 🎉
item.description: Open source has always been at the heart of what we do at Omnikon. Today, we're excited to share a milestone that means a lot to our entire community. Our repository, maintained by Sourabh, has...

item.title: Chrome Built-In AI APIs: A Hands-On Guide to Language Detection, Translation, Summarization and Writing Assistance
item.description: Introduction Chrome's Built-In AI APIs allow applications to perform selected AI workloads directly within the browser. Unlike traditional AI integrations, developers do not need to deploy or...

item.title: The smartest model lost — and it just redrew the 2026 AI race
item.description: The most interesting model comparison of 2026 isn't a benchmark table. It's a product exec quietly changing the question everyone asks about models — and getting a completely different ranking as...

item.title: The Paintbrush Paradox: Why the Monolithic Era of AI Is Crumbling
item.description: Over the past week, two narratives have been colliding everywhere I look. On one side, there's panic. AI is expected to replace marketers, engineers, and entire categories of knowledge work almost...

item.title: Salesforce Education Cloud: A Modern Alternative to EDA
item.description: Executive Summary The Salesforce Education Data Architecture (EDA) has served educational institutions well for over a decade as a free, community-supported managed package. However, with the 2023...

item.title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
item.description: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave...

item.title: The floatable, powerful Soundcore Boom 2 speaker is over half off
item.description: Bluetooth speakers with big sound and great features are hard to find for under $100, with most offerings being some variation of the same basic (and often small) design. Thankfully, through July...

item.title: Allstate accuses Broadcom of auditing it because it quit VMware, CA
item.description: Broadcom accuses Allstate of dodging VMware audits.

item.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
item.description: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What...

item.title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
item.description: You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update,...

item.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
item.description: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI...

item.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
item.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

item.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
item.description: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required...

item.title: Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description: Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API....

item.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
item.description: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

item.title: Anthropic Wants You to Pay Up for Claude Fable 5
item.description: Claude subscribers must soon pay usage-based fees to access Anthropic’s best consumer AI model—a sign that the golden era of AI subscriptions is ending.

item.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
item.description: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

item.title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as...

item.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
item.description: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part...

item.title: Google’s Nest Thermostat has hit its best price of the year
item.description: If you’re looking for a relatively affordable way to cut down on cooling costs, Google’s Nest Thermostat can help. It’s packed with smart controls and energy-saving features, and right now it’s on...

item.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
item.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

item.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
item.description: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the...

item.title: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
item.description: Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in a blog post on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means...

item.title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
item.description: About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in regulatory drama - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has received the...

item.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description: GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor...
